summarization.a: The figure shows the spectral response of Al-N after XeF2 treatment, with a fitted curve overlaid. Also, Al-N bond is highlighted at 73.6 eV.
summarization.b: The figure shows the spectral response of Al-F after BCl3 treatment, with a fitted curve overlaid. Also, Al-F bond is highlighted at 77.0 eV.

data_extraction.a:
| B. E. (eV) | Counts |
| --- | --- |
| 85 | - |
| 80 | peak at 77 eV, 78 eV|
| 75 | peak at 74 eV |
| 70 | peak at 73 eV |

a.question: What information can be obtained from the XPS spectra?
a.answer: The XPS technique is used to study the chemical state of the Al on the AlN surface during the ALE cycle.

a.question: How does the number of species change after exposure to XeF2 and BCl3?
a.answer: After XeF2 exposure, 4 species are present on the surface.

b.question: Which XPS spectra shows only one species?
b.answer: The subfigure b, where the AlN peak can be observed at 73.6 eV.

b.question: What performance advantage can be seen in the figure after BCl3 exposure?
b.answer: Exposure to BCl3 results in the removal of AlF3 from the surface, leaving only AlN.
